items.title: GPT-5.6: Frontier intelligence that scales with your ambition
items.summary: OpenAI announces GPT-5.6, a new frontier intelligence model designed to offer enhanced capabilities and scalability for various applications.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
items.summary: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, accusing the AI research company of stealing trade secrets, marking a significant legal escalation in the AI industry.

items.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
items.summary: Microsoft 365 Copilot has integrated OpenAI's newly released GPT-5.6 as its preferred model, indicating a major upgrade in its AI-powered productivity features.

items.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
items.summary: Meta has removed a controversial AI feature from Instagram following user backlash, highlighting the challenges of integrating AI into social platforms.

items.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
items.summary: Anthropic introduces Cowork, a new Claude Desktop agent designed to interact directly with user files without requiring any coding, aiming to enhance productivity for a broader audience.

items.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
items.summary: Salesforce has launched a new Slackbot AI agent, intensifying its competition with Microsoft and Google in the rapidly expanding workplace AI market.

items.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
items.summary: Clem Delangue, CEO of Hugging Face, emphasizes the increasing importance of open-source AI in the current technological landscape, advocating for collaborative development.

items.title: Video-Oasis: Rethinking Evaluation of Video Understanding
items.summary: Researchers introduce Video-Oasis, a new framework that re-evaluates and aims to improve the assessment of video understanding models.

items.title: Why Can't I Open My Drawer? Mitigating Object-Driven Shortcuts in Zero-Shot Compositional Action Recognition
items.summary: This research explores methods to mitigate object-driven shortcuts in AI models for zero-shot compositional action recognition, improving their ability to understand complex actions.

items.title: Every AI provider fails in its own way. I stopped checking status codes and built an error model instead.
items.summary: A developer shares insights on building a robust error model to handle the diverse failure modes of various AI providers, moving beyond simple status code checks.

items.title: Infinite Worlds with Versatile Interactions
items.summary: A new paper introduces a system capable of generating infinite virtual worlds with diverse and versatile interaction possibilities, pushing boundaries in generative AI.

items.title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
items.summary: UniClawBench is presented as a universal benchmark designed to comprehensively evaluate the performance of proactive AI agents in real-world task scenarios.

items.title: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation
items.summary: This research introduces a benchmark for evaluating AI's ability to reason about scientific lineage and generate new ideas based on historical scientific development.

items.title: Make AI Agents See Your Website
items.summary: This article provides guidance on how to optimize websites to be effectively interpreted and utilized by AI agents, enhancing their interaction capabilities.

items.title: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models
items.summary: LongE2V introduces a novel approach using video diffusion models for long-horizon event-based video reconstruction, prediction, and frame interpolation.
